ddls:
CREATE TABLE "energy_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each metric entry */,
  "date_time" TEXT /* Date and time the metric was recorded */,
  "cpid_value" REAL /* CPID sensor value (e.g., temperature) */,
  "cll_ds_lvl" REAL /* CLL sensor level (e.g., temperature level) */,
  "cll_us_lvl" REAL /* CLL US sensor level (e.g., upper limit level) */,
  "accl_gact" REAL /* ACCL GACT sensor value (e.g., actuator status) */,
  "cpt_ds_press" REAL /* CPT DS pressure sensor value */,
  "electric_heat_setpoint" INTEGER /* Electric heat load setpoint */,
  "electric_heat_readback" REAL /* Electric heat load readback value */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device that recorded the metric */,
  "sensor_type" TEXT /* Type of the sensor (e.g., temperature, pressure) */,
  "data_quality" TEXT /* Quality of the data collected (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the data (e.g., sensor, manual input) */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Any additional notes or comments */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_energy_metrics_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "devices" (
  "device_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each device */,
  "device_name" TEXT /* Name of the device */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the device within the facility */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date when the device was installed */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of the device (e.g., temperature sensor, pressure sensor) */,
  "model" TEXT /* Model of the device */,
  "manufacturer" TEXT /* Manufacturer of the device */,
  "serial_number" TEXT /* Serial number of the device */,
  "calibration_date" TEXT /* Date of the last calibration */,
  "calibration_due" TEXT /* Date of the next calibration due */,
  "maintenance_status" TEXT /* Current maintenance status (e.g., good, needs attention) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username used for login */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., manager, technician) */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the user */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the user */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the related metric entry */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., threshold exceeded) */,
  "alert_message" TEXT /* Detailed message about the alert */,
  "alert_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the alert was triggered */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity of the alert (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "resolved" INTEGER /* Whether the alert has been resolved */,
  "resolved_by" TEXT /* Who resolved the alert */,
  "resolved_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the alert was resolved */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Any additional notes or comments */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_metric_id FOREIGN KEY ("metric_id") REFERENCES energy_metrics ("metric_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "setpoints" (
  "setpoint_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each setpoint */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the setpoint (e.g., Electric Heat Load Setpoint) */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the setpoint */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the setpoint (e.g., °C, kW) */,
  "setpoint_type" TEXT /* Type of the setpoint (e.g., temperature, pressure) */,
  "setpoint_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the setpoint */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the setpoint */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the setpoint was created */,
  "last_modified_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last modified the setpoint */,
  "last_modified_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the setpoint was last modified */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("setpoint_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "historical_data" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each historical entry */,
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the related metric entry */,
  "average_value" REAL /* Average value for the time period analyzed */,
  "max_value" REAL /* Maximum value during the time period */,
  "min_value" REAL /* Minimum value during the time period */,
  "analysis_date" TEXT /* Date of the analysis */,
  "time_period" TEXT /* Time period for the analysis (e.g., daily, weekly, monthly) */,
  "analysis_type" TEXT /* Type of analysis (e.g., average, max, min) */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Any additional notes or comments */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user receiving the notification */,
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the related alert */,
  "notification_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the notification was sent */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the notification (e.g., sent, read) */,
  "notification_type" TEXT /* Type of notification (e.g., email, SMS) */,
  "sent_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who sent the notification */,
  "sent_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the notification was sent */,
  "read_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the notification was read */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_alert_id FOREIGN KEY ("alert_id") REFERENCES alerts ("alert_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role (e.g., admin, technician) */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions associated with this role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the role */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the role */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the role was created */,
  "last_modified_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last modified the role */,
  "last_modified_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the role was last modified */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "user_role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user-role mapping */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role assigned to the user */,
  "assigned_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the role was assigned */,
  "assigned_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who assigned the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_roles_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_roles_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "maintenance_logs" (
  "maintenance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each maintenance log */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device that was maintained */,
  "maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date when the maintenance was performed */,
  "performed_by" TEXT /* Name of the person who performed the maintenance */,
  "maintenance_details" TEXT /* Details of the maintenance performed */,
  "maintenance_type" TEXT /* Type of maintenance (e.g., calibration, repair) */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the maintenance (e.g., completed, in progress) */,
  "cost" REAL /* Cost of the maintenance */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Any additional notes or comments */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("maintenance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_maintenance_logs_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "report_name" TEXT /* Name of the report */,
  "generated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who generated the report */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date the report was generated */,
  "content" TEXT /* Content of the report */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of report (e.g., daily, weekly, monthly) */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the report (e.g., draft, final) */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Any additional notes or comments */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_generated_by FOREIGN KEY ("generated_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
